content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/virtualmachines/details/tabs/configuration/storage/components/tables/disk/DiskList.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 3 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 54
Rule [family:Label]:
## Label Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx
  <Label closeBtn={...} closeBtnAriaLabel={...} closeBtnProps={...} color={...} editableProps={...} href={...} icon={...} isClickable={...} />
```

Props that stay on <Label>: closeBtn, closeBtnAriaLabel, closeBtnProps, color, editableProps, href, icon, isClickable, isCompact, isDisabled, isEditable, onClick, onClose, onEditCancel, onEditComplete, render, status, textMaxWidth, tooltipPosition, variant

Prop type changes:
  color: 'blue' | 'cyan' | 'green' | 'orange' | 'purple' | 'red' | 'grey' | 'gold' -> 'blue' | 'teal' | 'green' | 'orange' | 'purple' | 'red' | 'orangered' | 'grey' | 'yellow'
  color: 'blue' | 'cyan' | 'green' | 'orange' | 'purple' | 'red' | 'grey' | 'gold' -> 'blue' | 'teal' | 'green' | 'orange' | 'purple' | 'red' | 'orangered' | 'grey' | 'yellow'
  variant: 'outline' | 'filled' -> 'outline' | 'filled' | 'overflow' | 'add'
  variant: 'outline' | 'filled' -> 'outline' | 'filled' | 'overflow' | 'add'

Deprecated -> v6 migration:
  Old import: @patternfly/react-core
  New import: @patternfly/react-core
Matching props:
New props on replacement:
  closeBtn: React.ReactNode
  closeBtnProps: any
  color: 'blue' | 'teal' | 'green' | 'orange' | 'purple' | 'red' | 'orangered' | 'grey' | 'yellow'
  editableProps: any
  href: string
  icon: React.ReactNode
  isClickable: boolean
  isCompact: boolean
  isDisabled: boolean
  isEditable: boolean
  onClose: (event: React.MouseEvent) => void
  onEditCancel: (event: KeyboardEvent, previousText: string) => void
  onEditComplete: (event: MouseEvent | KeyboardEvent, newText: string) => void
  render: ({
    className,
    content,
    componentRef
  }: {
    className: string;
    content: React.ReactNode;
    componentRef: any;
  }) => React.ReactNode
  status: 'success' | 'warning' | 'danger' | 'info' | 'custom'
  variant: 'outline' | 'filled' | 'overflow' | 'add'
Removed props (no replacement equivalent): badge, component, isOverflowChip, isReadOnly, ouiaId

Incidents found in this file:
  Line 54: [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-label-label-labelprops-d-ts-label-variant-type-changed]
    componentName: Label
    propName: variant
    propValue: filled
    Fix strategy:
      Strategy: PropTypeChange
      Component: Label
      Prop: variant
      From: 'filled' | 'outline'
      To: 'add' | 'filled' | 'outline' | 'overflow'
  Line 64: (same rule semver-packages-react-core-src-components-label-label-labelprops-d-ts-label-variant-type-changed)
    componentName: Label
    propName: variant
    propValue: filled
    Fix strategy:
      Strategy: PropTypeChange
      Component: Label
      Prop: variant
      From: 'filled' | 'outline'
      To: 'add' | 'filled' | 'outline' | 'overflow'

Code contexts:
  (line 54):
   53                isOpen={isOpen}
   54                onClose={onClose}
   55                onSubmit={onDiskUpdate || updateDisks}

  (line 64):
   63            <ListPageFilter
   64              data={data}
   65              hideLabelFilter



Code context:
```
(no snippet)
```

### Fix 2
Lines: 52, 63
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-label]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Label> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Label> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Label variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Label>
  After:  <Label variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Label> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Label><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Label>
  After:  <Label icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Label>

Incident context:
  componentName: Label
  parentName: StackItem

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Label
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 52):
   51              <DiskModal
   52                createDiskSource={diskSource}
   53                isOpen={isOpen}

  (line 63):
   62          <FlexItem>
   63            <ListPageFilter
   64              data={data}

```

### Fix 3
Lines: 36, 52, 62
Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-stackitem-component]:
Component <StackItem> has a new prop `component` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <StackItem> and determine if any should be moved to the `component` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <StackItem>{content} other children</StackItem>
  After:  <StackItem component=>other children</StackItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: StackItem
  parentName: Stack

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: StackItem
Prop: component
To: React.ReactNode

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 36):
   35    const { createModal } = useModal();
   36    const columns = useDiskColumns();
   37    const [disks, sourcesLoaded, loadError] = useDisksTableData(vm, vmi);

  (line 52):
   51              <DiskModal
   52                createDiskSource={diskSource}
   53                isOpen={isOpen}

  (line 62):
   61        <Flex>
   62          <FlexItem>
   63            <ListPageFilter

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/virtualmachines/details/tabs/configuration/storage/components/tables/disk/DiskList.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix against the actual file content:

### Fix 1 Analysis
The rule mentions `Label` component with `variant="filled"` at lines 54 and 64. Looking at the actual file:
- Line 54: `onClose={onClose}` — this is inside a `<DiskModal>`, not a `<Label>`
- Line 64: `data={data}` — this is inside a `<ListPageFilter>`, not a `<Label>`

There is **no `<Label>` component** in this file at all. The line numbers from the incidents don't match any Label usage. This fix doesn't apply to this file.

### Fix 2 Analysis
The rule is about moving icon children in `<Label>` to the `icon` prop. Again, there is **no `<Label>` component** in this file. The code contexts shown in the fix match lines in this file (DiskModal, ListPageFilter), but none of them involve `<Label>`. This fix doesn't apply.

### Fix 3 Analysis
The rule is about `<StackItem>` having a new `component` prop. Looking at the file, there is **no `<StackItem>` component** in this file. There's no `Stack` import either. The code contexts reference lines in this file but none involve `StackItem`. This fix doesn't apply.

All three fixes reference components (`Label`, `StackItem`) that do not exist in this file. The incident line numbers and code contexts correspond to completely different components (`DiskModal`, `ListPageFilter`, `FlexItem`). These appear to be false-positive rule matches — the scanner likely matched on something unrelated.

No changes are needed to this file.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Label variant type change): **Could not apply** — No `<Label>` component exists in this file. The incident lines (54, 64) correspond to `<DiskModal>` and `<ListPageFilter>` elements, not `<Label>`.

2. **Fix 2** (Label icon children-to-prop): **Could not apply** — No `<Label>` component exists in this file. No icon-as-children pattern to migrate.

3. **Fix 3** (StackItem component prop): **Could not apply** — No `<StackItem>` component exists in this file. No `Stack`/`StackItem` imports present.
